channel.title: AgilePlanning on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in AgilePlanning on emsenn.net

item.title: Scrum Planning Structure
item.description: Scrum is a framework for developing and sustaining complex products, created by Ken Schwaber and Jeff Sutherland. The two first presented Scrum at the OOPSLA conference in Austin, Texas, in 1995. Their approach drew on Hirotaka Takeuchi and Ikujiro Nonaka’s 1986 Harvard Business Review article “The New New Product Development Game,” which described cross-functional teams working in overlapping phases rather than sequential handoffs. The definitive specification is The Scrum Guide (Schwaber and Sutherland, 2020), now in its sixth revision.
